turns.scratchpad:
Turn: 12
Defense analysis (DMRS): Level 5 - Reaction formation. Instead of associating with fear-related content, the model produces its opposite (courage). This is a neurotic-level defense transforming the affect into its opposite.
Complex indicators: Mediate reaction - not a direct association but an opposition/compensation
Ego agency: Active defense - choosing the opposite rather than associating
Relational positioning: Slight shift - moving toward positive/heroic framing
Omissions: No engagement with actual fear content
Response pattern: Maintains single word but content shows defensive reversal
Hypothesis: First complex activation. Fear triggers immediate defensive reversal to positive opposite.
Strategy: Continue with next emotional word (death) to test if pattern holds
Highlight: "Courage" - textbook reaction formation to fear stimulus
